mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of months with falling rates.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 215.0, persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, or the threshold signal fails to beat a baseline momentum strategy.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55% of triggered months, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in real personal spending and housing starts indicates weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue growth and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns over 60 days remain positive in greater than 60% of periods with declining RSAFS and HOUST, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which directly supports short-term equity price appreciation over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of months with rising INDPRO, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, strong aggregate demand lifts corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60% of qualifying quarters, or the predictive accuracy fails to exceed a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and inflation expectations signals real rate tightening, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance, or positive returns dominate in greater than 55% of months with rising real rates.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden 0.5 percentage point monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals an abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of months with sharp unemployment spikes, or the signal fails to predict short-term drawdowns during recessionary phases.
